items.description: The United States and Iran continued exchanging strikes on Saturday as tensions over control of the Strait of Hormuz escalated. US Central Command carried out a seventh consecutive night of attacks, targeting military infrastructure and maritime capabilities, while Iran launched strikes on neighbouring Gulf states. items.title: Ethiopian Drill explodes on the streets of Addis Ababa
items.description: Last year, the aggressive genre of Drill could only be heard on social media in Ethiopia. But over the last few months the fast-paced, often matched with violent lyrics, style of hip hop has exploded on the streets of Addis Ababa, taking over mainstream bars and nightclubs. For some, its rapid popularity is due to Gen Z's lack of political and economic certainty. But Ethiopia remains a very conservative country, and not everyone is a fan of Drill. This report by our correspondent Tom Canetti.

items.title: Canada wildfires affect air quality before World Cup final
items.description: More than 800 wildfires are burning across Canada, including around 190 in northern Ontario, with the surge in fires largely attributed to climate change. Donald Trump has blamed Canada for its forest management and threatened additional tariffs. For more, we're joined by Akaash Maharaj, Director of Policy at Nature Canada.

items.title: French MPs approve assisted-dying law
items.description: On Wednesday, French lawmakers approved a bill that will create a legal right to assisted dying for adults with incurable illnesses. The issue has been championed by President Emmanuel Macron but has been highly contentious politically. Still - opinion polls suggest that a large majority of the French population supports assisted dying. We speak with Paul Smith, Head of modern languages at the University of Nottingham and Associate Professor in French and Francophone Studies.

items.title: Fight or Flight: Trump administration subpoenas journalists over Air Force One reporting
items.description: This week France 24's media show "Scoop" looks at Donald Trump's latest attempt to intimidate the press. The New York Times broke the story that the Qatari-donated Air Force One had security concerns. It was clearly newsworthy: the US President flying on a plane that was less secure than another that was available to him. The administration responded by issuing subpoenas to the journalists who wrote the article. Our guest is Will Creeley from the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression.

items.title: Landslide in China's Chongqing city kills 8, leaves 34 missing
items.description: Water, electricity and gas supplies were cut off within a 1-km (0.6-mile) radius of the landslide to prevent further disruptions. Over 800 rescuers were on the site

items.title: U.S. lawmaker urges Congress to review arms sales to India
items.description: Congressman Jim McGovern, co-chair of the Tom Lantos Human Rights Commission, also called on Congress to pass the bipartisan and bicameral Transnational Repression Policy Act

items.title: Nigeria repatriates 1,490 nationals from South Africa after xenophobic violence
items.description: Nigeria has completed the voluntary repatriation of its citizens from South Africa after weeks of xenophobic attacks and anti-immigration protests, the Nigerian foreign ministry said on Thursday.

items.title: US judge won't block Meta from laying off workers who filed AI discrimination lawsuit
At least 20 killed in jihadist attacks in eastern DRC
items.description: At least 20 people were killed this week in three attacks by suspected jihadist fighters in eastern DR Congo, according to a report from RFI.

items.title: Billionaire US envoy faces Venice protests amid yacht tour
items.description: Hundreds protested the arrival of billionaire and Trump-appointed US Ambassador Tilman Fertitta in Venice. The ambassador's stop — on his personal yacht — is part of a tour marking 250 years of American independence.

items.title: Lawyers clash in Maradona death trial, forcing hearing to halt
items.description: Judges suspend proceedings and warn they could remove counsel from the case after a heated confrontation between rival lawyers nearly turns physical. Leer más

items.title: Tensions as Guinea-Bissau freezes ties with Cape Verde over claims of interference
items.description: Praia earlier this week requested that Bissau swiftly release the country’s main opposition leader Domingos Simões Pereira.

items.title: Memorial unveiled for Romanian diplomat who saved Jews from Nazis
items.description: Florin Manoliu smuggled hundreds of protective passports past Nazi guards and helped expose Auschwitz's horrors to the Allied press; British Cemetery in Buenos Aires honours Romainian diplomat who found refuge in Bahía Blanca with new monument. Leer más

items.title: 'For the Malvinas, for Diego!' World Cup glee takes over Buenos Aires
items.description: Buenos Aires erupted into ecstasy as a famous semi-final victory over old foes England was secured. Leer más

items.title: British Embassy playfully defuses Argentina-England match tensions
items.description: Embassy's Instagram account shares a spoof "internal memo" instructing its community manager on how to behave – win or lose – in the wake of Wednesday’s World Cup semi-final. Leer más

items.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's next prime minister
items.description: Andy Burnham was confirmed as Keir Starmer's successor as British prime minister. The former Manchester mayor became a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

items.title: Air conditioner sparked deadly blaze at children's orphanage
items.description: Eleven people, including children, died in the fire that also injured 19 others.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: Mac Allister calls on Argentina to channel Maradona spirit in England World Cup clash
items.description: Argentina midfielder, who has played in England for two clubs over the last six seasons, urges his team to take inspiration from Diego as they seek to get the better of England. Leer más

items.title: Trump, Infantino give World Cup high praise, calling tournament a success
items.description: Trump called this World Cup “one of the all-time greatest sporting events in history,” adding that the tournament galvanized more than just the sporting world

items.title: 20 children killed in bus crash in Uganda
items.description: Twenty children and one adult were killed on Friday when their bus overturned in Uganda.

items.title: Messi received like royalty in New York ahead of World Cup final
items.description: Argentina's Lionel Messi has received rock star reception in New York City ahead of the World Cup final against Spain, but how much of a lasting impact will this tournament make in a crowded sporting market?

items.title: Pogacar wins Tour de France 14th stage to extend overall lead
items.description: Slovenia's cycling phenomenon Tadej Pogacar launched a devastating late attack Saturday to win the mountainous 14th stage of the Tour de France, extending his lead over Jonas Vingegaard.

items.title: Russian foreign minister hosts his Guinea-Bissau counterpart in Moscow
items.description: Russia's Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov welcomed his Guinea-Bissau’s counterpart Fatumata Jau in Moscow on Thursday.

items.title: How AI and satellites help fight wildfires
items.description: Wildfires are raging across Europe, Canada and beyond. To successfully fight these fires, speed is essential. A German startup uses satellite data and AI to spot fires early.

items.title: Space start-up launches India’s first commercial rocket designed for orbit
items.description: India's first space-tech unicorn Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched Vikram-1 on Saturday.

items.title: Messi: 'Historical context' makes World Cup win over England 'special'
items.description: Messi says he's looking forward to facing Spain in the World Cup final on Sunday; Skipper will face the country where he spent most of his career, at Barcelona. Leer más

items.title: Centuries-old hunt beckons the rain in western Senegal
items.description: In western Senegal, armed men on motorcycles took part in the 723rd edition of the "Miss de Diobaye” hunt, a centuries-old tradition believing to bring rain and protect the community.

items.title: Senate debate on ruling party’s land ownership bill pushed back after spat
items.description: Milei government fails to gather votes, postpones upper house debate over land ownership reform bill; Fourth delay for controversial bill that would eliminate restrictions on the foreign purchase of rural land; Comes after spat between Vice-President Villarruel and La Libertad Avanza Senate chief Patricia Bullrich. Leer más

items.title: U.S., Iran escalate strikes across West Asia
items.description: Iranian officials say recent U.S. strikes have killed dozens of people and wounded hundreds, with new casualties reported Friday, when the US military also acknowledged more injured service members.

items.title: East-West border death zone now Germany's biodiversity oasis
items.description: Many endangered species now live in the area that once formed the border between East and West Germany. Environment Minister Carsten Schneider recently visited the "Green Belt," and DW went along for the ride.

items.title: Ukraine lawmakers approve Koretskyi as new PM amid protests
items.description: It is President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's second Cabinet shakeup in a year. He faces criticism for dismissing reformist Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

items.title: China rescuers race to find trapped people after landslide
items.description: China launched a large-scale rescue operation after a landslide struck Pengshui County in Chongqing municipality on 17 July, burying part of a residential area and trapping an unknown number of people.

items.title: Algerian PM among the mourners as victims of orphanage fire laid to rest
items.description: Eleven people killed in a fire at an Algerian orphanage were laid to rest on Thursday at a ceremony in Bakari, on the outskirts of Algiers.

items.title: Control, threats, disfiguring surgery: My life inside Jeffrey Epstein's 'cult'
items.description: "Anya" gives the BBC a rare account of how sex-criminal financier Epstein lured and abused his “assistants”.

items.title: Irrepressible Argentina produce the goods in epic win over England
items.description: While the Albiceleste have celebrated epic knockout wins plenty of times in the recent past, few would have tasted sweeter than this most recent success. Leer más

items.title: Rescuers race to find survivors in rain after landslip in China kills at least eight
items.description: The landslide contained about 18,000 cubic meters of rocks and debris, and the largest single rock was around 3,000 cubic meters, Wang Chuanjun, head of Planning and Natural Resources in Pengshui County, told a news conference on July 17

items.title: Senegal: Faye, ex-president Macky Sall hold high-stakes meeting
items.description: Senegal's ex-president Macky Sall, who is campaigning to become the next UN secretary-general, held a meeting in Dakar Friday with his successor, a visit criticised by the former leader's detractors

items.title: Master and apprentice as Spain, Argentina coaches meet in World Cup final
items.description: Nine years ago, Argentina's coach was just another former professional player pursuing a career in coaching, studying for his pro-licence on the outskirts of Madrid. His teacher on the course? Luis de la Fuente. Leer más

items.title: France-England live: Les Bleus' humiliation deepens as Saka scores twice
items.description: France and England meet in the third-place playoff at the World Cup in Miami, with Didier Deschamps preparing for his final match as France manager after 14 years at the helm. Les Bleus and the Three Lions seek redemption following their semi-final heartbreak. Follow our liveblog for minute-to-minute commentary.

items.title: Taco Bell lettuce tied to diarrhea-causing parasite outbreak
items.description: US health officials linked iceberg lettuce served at Taco Bell to an outbreak of the parasite cyclospora, which causes “explosive” diarrhea. Health officials warn that the contamination may go beyond the fast-food chain.

items.title: Selección's never-say-die spirit secures place in World Cup final
items.description: Lautaro Martínez scores 92nd-minute winner as reigning champions Argentina pull off a sensational comeback over arch rivals England; Dramatic 2-1 victory sets up a final with European champions Spain and sends a nation onto the streets. Leer más

items.title: India launches first hydrogen-powered passenger train to cut emissions
items.description: India launches its first hydrogen-powered passenger train, NaMo Green Rail, boosting clean rail transport and cutting diesel emissions.

items.title: Messi eyes glorious farewell as Spain, Argentina clash in World Cup final
items.description: Holders Argentina are bidding to become the first team in 64 years to successfully defend the title, in what likely to be the final World Cup match of captain Lionel Messi's career. Leer más

items.title: Indian police shift Cockroach Party activist on hunger strike to hospital against his wish
items.description: Indian police on Saturday forcibly moved environmentalist Sonam Wangchuk to hospital after his health deteriorated during a weeks-long hunger strike in New Delhi. Wangchuk, a prominent activist tied to the viral, opposition Cockroach Janata Party, is seeking education reforms. His family has denounced the “lack of transparency" and trust in Indian authorities.

items.title: Civil rights groups oppose meeting between Macky Sall and President Diomaye
items.description: A group condemning the repression under former President Macky Sall’s rule in Senegal has spoken out against his arrival in Dakar on Friday to meet the Senegalese Head of State, as part of his campaign to lead the UN.
